workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed this workflow's workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} tailored to {user_skill_level}
Execute this workflow directly from these instructions. Do not rely on an external skill file.
The goal is traceability after delivery: compare implemented work against the PRD, convert actionable gaps into td work, and stop.

step.action: Run `td usage --new-session` if this is a fresh context; otherwise run `td usage -q`
Run `td reviewable`, `td ready`, and `td status` to confirm the current queue state
Load project context from {project_context} if present
Load validation methodology from {validation_methodology_file} if present so evidence expectations stay consistent
Load the PRD, epics/stories, and related planning artifacts from the BMAD workspace

check.if: reviewable or ready td issues exist
check.output: Execution work is not drained yet. Run `/bmad:td:next-step` first, then return to `/bmad:td:validate-prd` after the queue is clear.
No PRD was found, so PRD traceability cannot be completed with confidence. Recommend creating or restoring the PRD before using this workflow.

step.action: Read the PRD completely, including sharded sections when present
Extract each functional requirement, non-functional requirement, constraint, and explicitly testable acceptance expectation
Trace each requirement to available evidence in epics, stories, td issues, commits, changed files, and tests
Classify each requirement as covered, partial, missing, duplicate, or intentionally deferred
Call out any requirement that exists in the PRD but is absent from stories or absent from delivered implementation evidence
Review existing td issues first to avoid creating duplicates for known gaps
For each actionable missing or partial requirement, create or reuse a td task with requirement references in the title or body
Attach each new task to the most relevant story or epic when possible; otherwise create standalone follow-up work with enough context to continue execution
Set priority by impact: blockers, security, compliance, or data-loss gaps highest; core user flow gaps next; quality and polish gaps lower

check.action: Record that PRD coverage validation passed with no new td tasks required

step.action: Report which artifacts were inspected and summarize the requirement coverage outcome
List requirements classified as partial or missing, grouped by severity
List td tasks created or reused for each actionable gap
If new td tasks were created, state that `/bmad:td:next-step` can continue execution from the new queue
If no new td tasks were needed, state that PRD traceability validation completed and note any residual risks or follow-up recommendations
